config.note: targetMinutes drives how many questions are sampled. Lower them for a quick mock, raise them for a full-length one.

roundPurpose.round1: Recruiter / HR screen: motivation, fit, background.

international: Why do you want to work in this country specifically?
What is your current work authorisation or visa status?
Will you need visa sponsorship now or in the future?
Tell me about a time you adapted to a new culture or work environment.
Have you worked in an English-speaking, multicultural team before? How did it go?
How do you keep improving your professional English?
What attracted you to studying or living here?
How do you handle communication differences across cultures?
Are you comfortable with the local work style and pace?
Do you see yourself staying here long-term?

marketing.label: Marketing (Coordinator / Digital / Analyst)
round1: Tell me about yourself and how you got into marketing.
Why are you interested in this marketing role?
Why do you want to work for our company and brand?
Walk me through your resume.
What do you know about our products and our target audience?
Which marketing channels are you most comfortable with?
Which marketing tools have you used, such as Google Analytics, Meta Ads, or HubSpot?
What is your favourite marketing campaign by any brand, and why?
How do you stay up to date with marketing trends?
Where do you think digital marketing is heading in the next few years?
What does a strong brand mean to you?
Describe your experience with social media marketing.
How comfortable are you working with data and analytics in marketing?
What are your salary expectations?
What are your strengths as a marketer?
What is an area you are working to improve?
How do you handle feedback on your creative work?
Do you prefer working on strategy or execution, and why?
What motivates you most in a marketing job?
How would your past colleagues describe you?
What kind of marketing content do you enjoy creating most?
Do you have any questions for us?
round2: Tell me about a successful campaign you ran, including the goal, your role, and the result.
Describe a campaign that underperformed and what you changed.
Tell me about a time you used data to change a marketing decision.
Describe a time you managed several deadlines or priorities at once.
Tell me about a time you had to persuade a stakeholder to back your idea.
Describe a time you worked with a difficult team member or client.
Tell me about a time you took initiative on a project no one asked you to do.
Describe a time you had to deliver results within a very limited budget.
Tell me about a creative idea you championed from start to finish.
Describe a project that failed and what you learned from it.
Tell me about a time you had to learn a new tool or platform quickly.
Describe a time you received tough feedback and how you responded.
Tell me about a time you collaborated with sales, design, or product.
Describe a time you had to hit an aggressive growth target.
Tell me about a time you spotted an opportunity others missed.
Describe a time you made a decision without complete information.
Tell me about a time you handled a brand or PR problem.
Describe a time you improved a process or made something more efficient.
Tell me about a time your idea was rejected and how you handled it.
Describe a time you went above and beyond to get a result.
Tell me about a time you managed a campaign end to end.
Describe a time you had to balance creativity with business goals.
round3: How would you launch our product in a new market? Walk me through your plan.
How do you measure a campaign's success, and which KPIs matter most?
Explain CAC, CLV, and ROAS, and how they relate to each other.
Our website has high traffic but low conversions. How would you diagnose and fix it?
You have a 10,000 dollar budget. How would you allocate it across channels and why?
Estimate the potential market size for a new subscription coffee product.
How would you set up and interpret an A/B test on an email subject line?
How would you grow our Instagram following from 10,000 to 50,000?
How would you improve our email open and click-through rates?
A competitor just cut their prices. How should our marketing respond?
How would you build a content strategy for a B2B SaaS product?
Walk me through how you would plan a product launch timeline.
How would you segment our customer base for more effective targeting?
What is your approach to SEO for a brand-new website?
How would you measure brand awareness rather than just sales?
A campaign's ROAS is dropping over time. How do you investigate?
For a startup, how would you decide between paid ads and organic growth?
How would you use customer data to personalise our marketing?
Design a referral or loyalty program for our product.
How would you handle a campaign that goes viral for the wrong reasons?
Which metrics would you put on a marketing dashboard for the CEO?
How would you split budget between brand building and performance marketing?

data_analysis.label: Data Analysis (Data Analyst / Business Analyst)
round1: Tell me about yourself and your path into data analysis.
Why are you interested in this data analyst role?
Why do you want to work for our company?
Walk me through a data project from start to finish.
Which tools are you strongest in, such as SQL, Python, R, Excel, Tableau, or Power BI?
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate your SQL, and why?
Describe your experience with data visualisation.
How do you explain technical findings to non-technical stakeholders?
What kinds of data have you worked with, such as sales, web, survey, or financial?
What project are you most proud of, and why?
How do you make sure your analysis is accurate before sharing it?
How do you stay current with data tools and techniques?
What is your experience with statistics?
Have you worked with large datasets, and how large were they?
Do you prefer building dashboards or doing deep-dive analysis?
How do you prioritise when several stakeholders need data at the same time?
What does data-driven decision making mean to you?
Describe your experience cleaning messy data.
What are your strengths as an analyst?
What is an area you want to grow in?
What are your salary expectations?
Do you have any questions for us?
round2: Tell me about a time your analysis influenced a real business decision.
Describe a time you found an error in your own data or analysis.
Tell me about a time you delivered insights under a tight deadline.
Describe a time stakeholders disagreed with your findings.
Tell me about a time you had to work with very messy or incomplete data.
Describe a time you had to learn a new tool or technique quickly.
Tell me about a time you simplified a complex analysis for an audience.
Describe a time you automated or improved a manual process.
Tell me about a time you had to push back on a data request.
Describe a time you discovered an unexpected or surprising insight.
Tell me about a time you handled conflicting priorities from different teams.
Describe a time a recommendation you made turned out to be wrong.
Tell me about a time you collaborated with engineers or other analysts.
Describe a time you had to defend your methodology.
Tell me about a time you turned raw data into a clear story.
Describe a time you dealt with ambiguous requirements.
Tell me about a time your analysis saved the company time or money.
Describe a time you had to deliver bad news that came from the data.
Tell me about a time you owned a project end to end.
Describe a time you had to balance speed against accuracy.
Tell me about a time you helped or mentored a colleague with data.
Describe a time you had to question the data you were given.
round3: How would you write a SQL query to find the top 5 customers by total revenue?
Explain the difference between INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN.
How would you find duplicate rows in a table?
What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING?
Explain GROUP BY with an example.
How do you handle missing values in a dataset?
How do you detect and handle outliers?
Explain the difference between correlation and causation with an example.
What is a p-value, in plain English?
How would you design and interpret an A/B test?
A key metric dropped 20 percent last week. How do you investigate?
How would you measure the success of a new product feature?
Explain the difference between mean, median, and mode, and when each matters.
What is a normal distribution and why does it matter?
Describe a regression model and when you would use it.
Explain clustering and give a real business use case.
How would you design a dashboard for the sales team, and what would you include?
Estimate how many cups of coffee a busy city cafe sells in a day.
What is the difference between a database and a data warehouse?
How would you explain a confidence interval to a manager?
Walk me through how you would analyse customer churn.
How do you choose the right chart type for a given dataset?
What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
How do you make sure your analysis is trustworthy before you present it?

ai_ml.label: AI / Machine Learning (Data Scientist / ML Engineer)
round1: Tell me about yourself and what draws you to AI and machine learning.
Why are you interested in this AI or ML role?
Why do you want to work for our company?
Walk me through a machine learning project from start to finish.
Which ML frameworks and libraries do you use?
What programming languages are you strongest in?
Describe a model you have built and the problem it solved.
How do you stay up to date in such a fast-moving field?
What recent AI development or paper impressed you, and why?
How comfortable are you with the maths behind ML, such as linear algebra and statistics?
Have you deployed a model to production? Describe it.
What is your experience with deep learning versus classical machine learning?
Do you prefer research and experimentation, or engineering and deployment?
How do you approach a brand-new ML problem you have never seen before?
What is your experience with large language models or generative AI?
How do you think about AI ethics and responsibility?
What are your strengths as an ML practitioner?
What is an area you are actively learning right now?
Describe your experience working with data pipelines.
How do you handle a project where the data quality is poor?
What are your salary expectations?
Do you have any questions for us?
round2: Tell me about a time a model did not perform as expected. How did you debug it?
Describe a time you balanced model accuracy against a constraint like latency, cost, or interpretability.
Tell me about shipping a model together with engineering or product teams.
Describe a bias or fairness issue you encountered or anticipated.
Tell me about a time you explained a complex model to non-technical stakeholders.
Describe a time you had to learn a new technique for a project.
Tell me about a time your first approach failed and you had to pivot.
Describe a time you had to defend a modelling choice.
Tell me about a time you worked with messy or insufficient training data.
Describe a time you delivered a project under tight time constraints.
Tell me about a disagreement with a teammate on a technical approach.
Describe a time you improved an existing model or system.
Tell me about a time you had to choose between a simple and a complex solution.
Describe a time you caught a data leakage or evaluation mistake.
Tell me about a time you had to prioritise between competing experiments.
Describe a project where business impact mattered more than model accuracy.
Tell me about a time you had to communicate uncertainty in your results.
Describe a time you mentored someone or shared technical knowledge.
Tell me about a time you took an idea from prototype to production.
Describe a time a stakeholder had unrealistic expectations of AI.
Tell me about a time you debugged a problem with a model already in production.
Describe a time you balanced experimentation against delivery deadlines.
round3: Explain overfitting and the bias-variance tradeoff.
How do you prevent overfitting?
How do you evaluate a classification model, and which metrics do you use and when?
Explain precision versus recall and when you would prioritise each.
What is ROC-AUC and what does it tell you?
How do you handle an imbalanced dataset?
Explain cross-validation and why it matters.
Walk me through building a churn-prediction system end to end.
Explain how a random forest works.
Explain gradient boosting at a high level.
What is the difference between bagging and boosting?
Explain how a neural network learns, including forward pass and backpropagation.
What is regularisation, such as L1 and L2, and why use it?
Explain the difference between supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.
How would you approach feature engineering for a tabular problem?
What is data leakage and how do you avoid it?
What is retrieval-augmented generation, or RAG, and when would you use it?
When would you choose fine-tuning, prompt engineering, or RAG?
How does the attention mechanism in a transformer work, at a high level?
How would you deploy a model and monitor it for drift?
Explain the difference between classification and regression with examples.
How would you reduce the size or latency of a model for production?
How would you approach reducing customer churn using machine learning, end to end?
How do you decide whether a problem actually needs ML, versus simpler rules?
